deferred: ERR-4b: order-submission path bubble-up — submit_live_order acquires a line on first use of an unsubscribed symbol and surfaces SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_REACHED through StructuredOrderError
Concrete SubscriptionLineCounter impl backed by ATP_MARKET_DATA_LINE_LIMIT and the live subscription set (owner: SRS-MD-001 / SRS-MD-007)
Concrete RecordValidator implementing the six SyRS SYS-77 rules (a..f) against equity and options records (owner: SRS-DATA-001..006 + SRS-DATA-013)
Quarantine storage backend that persists the rejected payload + reason + timestamp + source (owner: SRS-DATA-014 / SRS-DATA-015)
Notification dispatcher hookup that fans the per-record event into dashboard alert counts and SMTP/SMS reason summaries (owner: SRS-NOTIF-001 / SYS-77 alert clause)

Concrete PacingBudgetValidator that projects watchlist size × per-symbol request granularity against the IB pacing tier (SYS-31's 60-requests-per-10-minute ceiling) for the SYS-22b overnight window and the SYS-23 configured near-close window (owner: SRS-DATA-002 + SRS-DATA-004 + the orchestrator-side scheduler config)
Dashboard alert pane + notification dispatcher fan-out that surfaces SyRS SYS-55's 'until scope or window configuration is reduced' guidance to the operator (owner: SRS-NOTIF-001 / SRS-LOG-001)

Runtime 15-second identical-request throttle (SyRS SYS-31's second pacing clause) — orthogonal to the scheduling-time gate; belongs on the IB historical-data adapter request path, not on the scheduling-time validator (owner: SRS-DATA-002 + SRS-DATA-004 adapter implementation)
Concrete StrategyContainerRuntime backed by the Docker Engine API for the Phase 1 Proxmox VM target (owner: SRS-ARCH-004 + SRS-ORCH-002)

Concrete HealthCheckEventSink wired to the dashboard WebSocket alert pane + the SRS-LOG-001 audit trail + the SRS-NOTIF-001 dispatcher (owner: SRS-LOG-001 / SRS-NOTIF-001 / SyRS SYS-13 dashboard clause)

Container-runtime cooldown / back-off when an unresponsive container restart or an over-deadline destroy fails repeatedly (orthogonal to the gate; belongs on the concrete StrategyContainerRuntime implementation)

Concrete StrategyContainerRuntime that applies ResourceProfile to a real Docker container at create time (owner: SRS-ARCH-004 + SRS-ORCH-002 — same Docker driver entry deferred on the lifecycle contract). Until that lands, the gate validates ResourceProfile as DATA at the orchestrator boundary; no live Docker cgroup limit is applied. Same wired-but-empty shape ERR-2..6 + SRS-ORCH-001 shipped under.

Typed error surface on StrategyContainerRuntime lifecycle methods (create / stop / restart / destroy currently return unit, so a future Docker/cgroup driver cannot signal cgroup-creation failure or insufficient-host-resource refusal back to the launch gate). Owner: SRS-ORCH-001 trait-signature retrofit, paired with the Docker driver — adding the error surface only matters once a real runtime can produce the errors. The contract block already names this as a paired-feature concern.

Production caller of StrategyOrchestrator::launch — the env-var → launch-profile wire is built in this session (profile_for_mode_from_env reads ATP_*_STRATEGY_* via std::env::var, falls back to spec defaults, validates against catalogue bounds), but main.py is still a `pass` stub and no Rust orchestrator service binary invokes launch() yet. Owner: SRS-ARCH-001 production wiring (orchestrator service entry point) — once the binary lands, it calls profile_for_mode_from_env(mode) to populate StrategyLaunchRequest.profile.
Per-strategy override flow from the dashboard / REST API (owner: SRS-API-001 — REST/WebSocket override path layered on top of the env-var lookup once the API surface lands)

workload_event_sink_port.record_signature: fn record(&self, event: WorkloadAdmissionEvent) -> Result<(), WorkloadEventSinkError>

deferred: Concrete HostMemoryProbe backed by sysinfo / procfs / /proc/meminfo (owner: SRS-ARCH-004 + SRS-ORCH-003 — Phase 1 Proxmox VM target). Until that lands, the gate consumes the probe trait through stubs in tests; no real host memory is observed. The contract pin on `forbidden_imports_in_orchestrator_crate` prevents the orchestrator crate itself from depending on sysinfo — the concrete impl will live in a separate adapter crate.

Concrete WorkloadRegistry that lists active strategy containers, ingestion jobs, factor jobs, backtest runs, and Jupyter sessions (owner: SRS-ORCH-001 + SRS-ARCH-004 — same Docker driver entry deferred on the lifecycle contract). Until that lands, the registry is consumed through stubs.

Concrete WorkloadEventSink wired to the dashboard alert pane + SRS-LOG-001 audit trail + SRS-NOTIF-001 dispatcher. SRS-ORCH-003's acceptance criterion 'dashboard and notification alerts are emitted' is satisfied at the orchestrator boundary today — every refusal / eviction / probe-failure / registry-failure / termination-failure path issues a structured `WorkloadAdmissionEvent` through the typed port, and the 5 audit-event variants (Refused, Terminated, TerminationFailed, HostProbeFailed, RegistryListingFailed) discriminate the failure mode for the dashboard alert lane. Emission is best-effort by design: the gate calls `sink.record(...)` and does not abort or roll back the admission decision on a sink Err (the decision is irreversible once made). The typed `WorkloadEventSinkError` exists so a future caller wrapping the orchestrator can observe sink failures separately from admission outcomes without retrofitting the trait. Durable delivery semantics (bounded queue + retry, persistent journal, fan-out to a secondary channel) belong on the SRS-LOG-001 (audit log) + SRS-NOTIF-001 (notification dispatcher) + SyRS SYS-13 (dashboard) implementations — same deferred dispatcher block that consumes HealthCheckEventSink in the orchestrator-lifecycle contract.

Concrete failure variants on WorkloadTerminationError, HostMemoryProbeError, and WorkloadRegistryError. The gate already consumes all three port methods as Result<..., Error> and refuses safely on any failure (probe error → refuse; active() error → refuse; terminate() error → emit TerminationFailed + skip banking), but the error types today carry only a short reason string. Future variants — sysinfo refresh failed, /proc/meminfo unavailable, Docker engine timeout, cgroup permission denied, registry desynchronized, etc. — will be added when the concrete adapters land so the dashboard can render the specific failure cause.

Workload-agnostic admission envelope. SRS-ORCH-003 ships the SyRS SYS-57 hierarchy as a workload-agnostic data model (WorkloadPriority + WorkloadKind + RegisteredWorkload) but the admission gate currently accepts a StrategyLaunchRequest, which is the right shape for strategy admission (the orchestrator's primary domain — SRS-5.2). Non-strategy admission paths (ingestion-job, factor-pipeline-job, backtest-run, research-session) belong to their owning services and will introduce their own gate methods consuming the same WorkloadPriority library. The structural separation between request envelope and workload-priority is intentional, not drift — the priority parameter is decoupled from request.mode because SYS-57 ranks workloads categorically, not by strategy mode.

Production caller of StrategyOrchestrator::admit_workload — main.py is still a `pass` stub and no Rust orchestrator service binary invokes the gate. Owner: SRS-ARCH-001 production wiring (orchestrator service entry point).

Typed coupling of `admit_workload` to `launch`. Today the two gates are separate methods and `launch`'s Rustdoc states that calling it without first calling `admit_workload` bypasses the SyRS SYS-57 / SYS-58 enforcement. A future session will turn that 'must call' precondition into 'cannot fail to call' via a typestate (`admit_workload` returns an `AdmissionToken`; `launch` consumes the token) or a builder pattern. The typestate change is scoped to land when the production caller of `launch` is wired (same owner as the deferred entry above) so the parameter-list expansion happens once. Until then, the contract-level requirement is exercised by the integration + domain tests for SRS-ORCH-003 (codex critic: arch:admission-gate-bypass).

Gate-level capture and propagation of WorkloadEventSink::record errors. Today emission is best-effort (see WorkloadEventSink trait doc) — the gate does not capture or surface sink failures because the admission decision is irreversible once made. A future session will introduce a richer return shape (e.g. AdmissionOutcome { decision, sink_failures: Vec<...> } or a side-channel collector) once durable-delivery semantics are owned by the concrete SRS-NOTIF-001 dispatcher and gate-level retry / propagation becomes meaningful (codex critic: adapter:sink-error-discard).
Per-strategy / per-job override of the safety margin from the dashboard / REST API (owner: SRS-API-001 — REST/WebSocket override path layered on top of the env-var lookup once the API surface lands).

Concrete DeployedVersionRegistry implementation backed by a durable store (SQLite, sled, or the SRS-DATA-* state layer once the data layer lands). Today the trait is consumed only by spy implementations in tests; the production caller will land alongside the dashboard / REST API surface (owner: SRS-API-001 / SRS-UI-002).
Dashboard wiring (SyRS SYS-41) that renders the version_identifier on the active-strategy inventory. Owner: SRS-UI-002 + SRS-UI-001.
REST API endpoint (SyRS IF-9) that returns the version_identifier per strategy id. Owner: SRS-API-001.

Backtest result column carrying the strategy code version (SyRS SYS-21). The orchestrator's DeployedVersion record is the source of truth; the backtest pipeline (SRS-BT-001 / SRS-BT-009) will project it onto each backtest result row when those surfaces land.

Concrete failure variants on DeployedVersionRegistryError (durable-store outage / permission denied / mutex poisoning / file-system full). The reason string carries the cause for now; structured variants will be added when the concrete registry adapter lands.

Typed coupling of `launch` to the version_registry record (today emission is best-effort by design — a record failure does not abort the launch). Future durable-delivery semantics on the SRS-LOG-001 / SRS-NOTIF-001 dispatcher path may add gate-level capture of the registry-record Result for retry / propagation.

Source-hash compute path: where the SHA-256 over the strategy source files is calculated. Today the launch envelope receives the hash as a parameter (the caller computes it); the deferred work is the orchestrator helper that hashes the on-disk strategy bundle. Owner: SRS-ORCH-004 follow-up alongside the production orchestrator entry point.

SYS-2e IB paper account integration smoke (gated by ATP_RUN_INTEGRATION=1, owner: SRS-EXE-001 + the future paper-simulation engine). The in-process L7 stub parity test covers the same-strategy-source-runs-both-paths invariant; the real-IB-Gateway round-trip is deferred.

Concrete LiveIBStrategyContext and PaperSimStrategyContext drivers — only stub drivers live in tests/domain/ today; the real drivers land with SRS-EXE-001 and SRS-SIM-*. The parity invariant added here will naturally cover them once they subclass StrategyContext.
Performance-parity surface (SRS-SDK-004 callback latencies: <1000ms p95 live, <100ms p95 paper). Only the shape parity is enforced here; the latency parity rides on the real engines.

strategy_api_scheduler_contract.description: Trading-calendar-aware scheduling primitives for SRS-SDK-002 (SyRS SYS-6 / SYS-50 / SYS-51; StRS SN-1.09 / SN-1.19 / BG-1 / BG-7).
strategy_api_scheduler_contract.calendar_horizon_pin_rationale: exchange_calendars.get_calendar without explicit start/end uses a host-date-rolling default window (~20y backward, ~1y forward). Pinning to a fixed [2000-01-01, 2035-12-31] window makes session-validity decisions date-deterministic across runs so the same historical or scheduled query does not silently flip from valid to CalendarHorizonExceeded as the wall clock advances. Widen the window when SRS-BT-* needs older data or live scheduling exceeds 2035.
deferred: Live wall-clock fire loop is owned by SRS-EXE-001 + SRS-ORCH-* (orchestrator dispatches scheduled callbacks; this module resolves fire times).
Pre-market and after-hours market-data subscription routing is owned by SRS-MD-* (market-data subscription manager).

CBOE Options is aliased to XNYS — exchange_calendars ships no dedicated CBOE Options calendar (XCBF is CBOE Futures with a 16:15 ET close, wrong asset class). NYSE / NASDAQ / CBOE-Options share the SIFMA U.S.-equity-class regular-session holiday calendar in practice for 2026; the alias is correct for the strategy SDK's equity-class option workflows. CBOE-specific option-only early closes (e.g., quad-witching schedule adjustments that diverge from NYSE) are deferred. Owner: future feature that ships maintained CBOE Options session tables. Update _EXCHANGE_HANDLES in python/atp_strategy/calendar.py and re-target the L7 documented-alias test when a real handle lands.

strategy_api_subscriptions_contract.description: Single tradable asset class invariant for SRS-SDK-003 (SyRS SYS-5 single-tradable-class + SyRS SYS-64 structured-error contract; StRS SN-1.07 / BG-1 / BG-5). A strategy configured for equities or options may subscribe to both asset classes for analysis; order submission against the non-configured class raises AssetClassViolation. Identical contract for live IB execution and internal paper simulation per SRS-SDK-001 AC-14.
deferred: MARKET_DATA_STALE and SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT_REACHED structured-error types (per SyRS SYS-64) are owned by SRS-MD-002 (market-data line-limit enforcement) and SRS-MD-004 (stale-data order blocking); the SDK surface here exposes the asset-class guard only.

Live IB and internal paper simulation StrategyContext implementations are owned by SRS-EXE-001 / SRS-SIM-001 — they must call assert_asset_class before routing the order. The L7 domain test for SRS-SDK-003 ships a reference _RefStrategyContext that exercises this contract; production drivers re-use the SDK helper rather than reimplementing the comparison.

strategy_api_order_events_contract.description: SDK-surface contract for SRS-SDK-004 order event callback delivery (SyRS SYS-7 live-callback delivery + SYS-85 paper engine reuses the same IF-7 API surface + NFR-P4 p95 latency budgets; StRS SN-1.22 / SN-1.29 / BG-1). SCOPE: this contract covers the AC-required *field presence* half of SRS-SDK-004 ONLY. The NFR-P4 *latency proof* half (live <1,000 ms p95 from broker fill acknowledgement; paper <100 ms p95 from simulated fill) requires the real SRS-EXE-001 live dispatcher and SRS-SIM-001 paper dispatcher to exist and is gated on those features. Until then, SRS-SDK-004 stays passes:false in feature_list.json; the SDK surface here is the prerequisite production dispatchers will consume. The SDK ships the OrderEvent / OrderEventType surface plus an assert_order_event_payload guard Python dispatchers must call before invoking Strategy.on_order_event so user code can rely on the documented field-presence contract; Rust core runtime dispatchers apply the same field-presence rules locally per the AGENTS.md one-way dependency direction (Rust core must not depend on the Python SDK). This metadata block is the cross-language source of truth — the Python SDK constants LIVE_CALLBACK_LATENCY_P95_MS / PAPER_CALLBACK_LATENCY_P95_MS are the Python-side view kept in parity with required_live_callback_latency_p95_ms / required_paper_callback_latency_p95_ms below, and Rust dispatchers read those same keys directly (or from a generated Rust constants file derived from them). The four AC-named callback categories (FILL, PARTIAL_FILL, CANCELLED, REJECTED) must include fill_price, fill_quantity, and commission per docs/SRS.md row SRS-SDK-004; dispatchers populate explicit zeros on a never-filled cancel or reject and cumulative average / total on a partially-filled cancel or reject. Live and paper modes use the same delivery surface per SRS-SDK-001 AC-14.
deferred: Live IB execution dispatcher (broker fill acknowledgement -> assert_order_event_payload -> Strategy.on_order_event) is owned by SRS-EXE-001. End-to-end live p95 latency proof requires a running IB Gateway and is verified by SRS-EXE-001's contract / performance tests rather than the SDK contract here.

Internal paper simulation dispatcher (simulated fill -> assert_order_event_payload -> Strategy.on_order_event) is owned by SRS-SIM-001. End-to-end paper p95 latency proof requires a running simulation engine and is verified by SRS-SIM-001's contract / performance tests.

The L7 domain test for SRS-SDK-004 ships a reference _RefDispatcher exercising both the field-presence guard and a deterministic synthetic in-process latency measurement under the PAPER budget; production dispatchers re-use the SDK helper and budget constants rather than redefining them.

strategy_api_warmup_contract.description: Warm-up mechanism for SRS-SDK-005 (SyRS SYS-8 warm-up + NFR-R3 restart re-execution; StRS SN-1.23 / SC-18 200-bar warm-up / BG-1 / BG-2 / BG-7). The SDK ships a WarmupController + WarmupState lifecycle + assert_warmup_complete guard that every concrete dispatcher (live IB execution per SRS-EXE-001, internal paper simulation per SRS-SIM-001, backtest engine per SRS-BT-001) must drive before delivering the first executable bar. The AC ordering rule (historical bars replayed via Strategy.on_bar before any executable bar, indicator buffers and rolling windows initialised before any trading signal can be generated, on_warmup_complete fired exactly once at the boundary) is enforced at the SDK surface. The controller is fail-closed on a short historical replay: if HistoricalData.get_bars returns fewer than warmup_bars bars for any subscribed symbol the controller raises WarmupNotComplete, leaves WarmupState at IN_PROGRESS, does not invoke on_warmup_complete, and keeps the executable gate (assert_warmup_complete) closed. Identical surface in live and paper modes per SRS-SDK-001 AC-14. The cross-language source of truth lives here; Rust core runtime dispatchers re-implement the same gate locally per AGENTS.md dependency direction (no Rust -> Python SDK dependency).
strategy_api_warmup_contract.required_history_shortfall_behavior: raise WarmupNotComplete (StrategyAPIError); keep WarmupState at IN_PROGRESS; do not call Strategy.on_warmup_complete; keep assert_warmup_complete closed
deferred: Live IB execution dispatcher (live market-data stream gated on WarmupState.COMPLETE) is owned by SRS-EXE-001. The live dispatcher must construct WarmupController, invoke run() before subscribing to live executable bars, and call assert_warmup_complete at the executable boundary.
Internal paper simulation dispatcher (shared live market-data stream gated on WarmupState.COMPLETE) is owned by SRS-SIM-001. Mirror of the live path; the dispatcher must drive the same controller.

Backtest engine warm-up phase (historical replay window split between warm-up and evaluation regions) is owned by SRS-BT-001. Same controller drives the warm-up segment; executable bars begin after WarmupState.COMPLETE.

NFR-R3 restart re-execution of the warm-up mechanism is owned by SRS-EXE-005 (live persistence + restart) and SRS-SIM-004 (paper persistence + restart). On container restart the dispatcher constructs a fresh WarmupController and replays history; the SDK contract here is the same surface both restart paths consume.

The L7 domain test for SRS-SDK-005 ships a reference _RefDispatcher and _StubHistoricalData that exercise the AC end-to-end with a 200-bar warm-up + a 200-period SMA. The test proves: (a) all 200 historical bars hit on_bar before the first executable bar; (b) indicator buffers (SMA.is_ready) are True at the executable boundary; (c) order submission attempted during warm-up raises WarmupNotComplete; (d) on_warmup_complete fires exactly once.

strategy_api_indicators_contract.description: Built-in technical indicators for SRS-SDK-006 (SyRS SYS-35 + AC-6 + StRS SN-1.20 + C-9). The SDK ships SMA, EMA, RSI, MACD, BollingerBands, and ATR as thin glue wrappers around BOTH TA-Lib and pandas-ta, choosing the per-indicator canonical reference (see primary_backend_per_indicator: SMA and BollingerBands route through pandas-ta; EMA / RSI / MACD / ATR route through TA-Lib). SyRS AC-6 prohibits custom reimplementations of indicators available in these libraries; the wrappers contain buffering, dtype-conversion, and NaN -> None glue ONLY, with no arithmetic. Each indicator class implements atp_strategy.api.Indicator (value / is_ready / update). MACD additionally exposes MACDValue via .reading (macd, signal, histogram); BollingerBands exposes BollingerValue via .reading (middle, upper, lower). All six classes support incremental updates: a fresh Bar appended to an internal rolling deque triggers a recompute (TA-Lib for EMA/RSI/MACD/ATR; pandas-ta for SMA/BollingerBands) over the buffer and the wrapper exposes the last finite output. The L7 parity test additionally cross-checks every indicator against the OTHER library so AC-6 'wraps both' is verified end-to-end, not just at the import line. The same public surface is used in live IB execution (SRS-EXE-001), internal paper simulation (SRS-SIM-001), backtest (SRS-BT-001), and Jupyter research (SRS-RES-002) per SRS-SDK-001 AC-14. TA-Lib's C library is a mandatory native dependency (see docker/strategy-python.Dockerfile, docker/jupyter.Dockerfile, and .github/workflows/ci.yml install TA-Lib 0.6.4); the Python wrapper requires the C shared library on the loader path.
strategy_api_indicators_contract.primary_backend: mixed: pandas-ta for SMA / BollingerBands; TA-Lib for EMA / RSI / MACD / ATR
strategy_api_indicators_contract.parity_reference: the OTHER library — every indicator pins parity against both backends in the L7 test
strategy_api_indicators_contract.nan_to_none_convention: TA-Lib emits np.nan for warm-up positions where the indicator buffer is not yet full. Wrappers MUST convert the trailing NaN to Python None on .value and .reading so user strategy code does not need to special-case NaN. For compound indicators (MACD, BollingerBands) the whole reading is None if any component leg is NaN.
deferred: Extended indicator coverage (ADX, OBV, Stochastic, Ichimoku, etc.) is intentionally out of scope; SRS-SDK-006 names exactly SMA / EMA / RSI / MACD / BollingerBands / ATR. Future indicator additions follow the same wrap-talib-and-pandas-ta pattern enforced by this contract.
Strategy-author-facing documentation for the indicator surface (usage examples, edge-case guide, performance notes) is owned by SRS-SDK-009 (P1, docs).

Optimising for O(1) per-update cost is out of scope. The SRS-SDK-006 AC says 'support incremental updates', not 'O(1) per update'. O(period) recompute per bar via TA-Lib's C-level loop is acceptable and matches the prior pure-Python implementation's effective cost.

Documented seed-bar divergence between TA-Lib and pandas-ta on RSI / MACD / ATR (different Wilder/EMA seeding conventions) is not a bug. The L7 IndicatorPandasTaParityTest slices the first pandas_ta_seed_skip_bars_factor * period bars before comparing, and the divergence is documented in the indicators.py module docstring.

strategy_api_documentation_contract.description: User-facing documentation surface for SRS-SDK-009 (SyRS NFR-U2, StRS SN-1.01, C-1). The Python Strategy API ships with author-grade docstrings on every public name in atp_strategy.__all__, a README guide under python/atp_strategy/README.md that walks the full SDK surface, and a python/atp_strategy/examples/ subpackage containing self-contained runnable Strategy subclasses that demonstrate every documented capability end-to-end. The acceptance bar (per SRS-SDK-009 AC) is that a Python-proficient trader can author a new strategy using only the docstrings + README + example files, without ever needing to open python/atp_strategy/api.py or any other internal file. The contract block here is the cross-language source of truth for the tier system, README sections, and example modules; the evidence script tools/strategy_api_documentation_check.py and the L3/L7 tests under tests/test_strategy_api_documentation_contract.py and tests/domain/test_api_documentation.py enforce it.
anti_drift_rules: Every identifier referenced by a from atp_strategy import statement in any example module under python/atp_strategy/examples/ MUST appear in atp_strategy.__all__.

Every example module under python/atp_strategy/examples/ MUST import only from atp_strategy (the package facade), from atp_strategy.examples (sibling scaffolding such as the local _harness helper), or the Python standard library. Deep-path imports into platform internals (from atp_strategy.api import _x, from atp_strategy.warmup import _y, from atp_strategy.indicators import _z, from atp_strategy.scheduler import _w, from atp_strategy.calendar import _q) are forbidden because they tell strategy authors to read platform internals.
Every example module MUST define exactly one class that is a strict subclass of atp_strategy.Strategy.

No example module may contain conditional branches discriminating on the strings 'live', 'paper', or 'backtest' (case-insensitive) — SRS-SDK-001 AC-14 requires the same source to run in all three modes.
Every `python -m atp_strategy.examples.<name>` invocation referenced in python/atp_strategy/README.md MUST resolve to a module name in required_example_modules.

Every code fence (``>>> `` doctest or ```python``` fenced block) inside the docstring of any public symbol in atp_strategy.__all__ MUST import only from atp_strategy (the facade) and reference only names in __all__. Deep-path imports in docstring examples teach strategy authors that platform internals are the supported surface, contradicting the SRS-SDK-009 AC.

Every ```python``` code fence inside python/atp_strategy/README.md MUST import only from atp_strategy and reference only names in __all__. The same anti-internals rule applies to the README as to the docstrings.
deferred: Sphinx / mkdocs / readthedocs HTML rendering of the docstring + README surface is out of scope for SRS-SDK-009. The AC is satisfied by the in-source docstring + README + runnable examples surface; auto-rendered HTML can be added later without changing the SDK contract.

Doctest-style ``>>> `` execution via pytest --doctest-modules is intentionally NOT wired in. The author-facing convention chosen here is single-language ```python``` code fences in the README and example-runner verification in the L7 domain test; pytest --doctest-modules would constrain docstring style (mock everything, single-line scenes) without adding signal beyond the L7 example-runner.
Translation / internationalization of docstring content is out of scope; the platform is single-locale per StRS scope.

A formal style guide for future docstring additions (e.g., enforced Google-style or NumPy-style Args/Returns/Raises sections) is intentionally deferred. The contract here pins minimum docstring length per tier, a usable example fence on tier-1 names, and the runnable-example coverage rule — sufficient for the SRS-SDK-009 AC without forcing a particular section header format on the existing surface.
